full_prompt: Design a monolithic 6082-T6 marine-grade aluminum crossbeam for the student-designed cockpit of a collegiate energy-class electric catamaran benchmark using one-design catamaran hulls, respecting the benchmark rules: fossil-fuel ban, <=10 kWh stored energy budget, one-design catamaran hulls with a 2.0 m beam center-to-center between the two hulls. The crossbeam spans the two hulls, carries the helm, the driver seat, the 10 kWh battery pack, and the tiller-arm steering linkage; it is a single machined-and-extruded billet body (no welds, no bolted gussets). Geometry: straight tubular crossbeam 2000 mm long, 120 mm x 100 mm rectangular hollow section with 6 mm wall thickness, ends terminating in 300 mm x 200 mm flanged mounting pads (15 mm thick) each with six M10 (11 mm clearance) holes for bonding / bolting to the hull's deck inserts. Two integral top-facing mounting feet near the center (600 mm apart) for the battery tray: each foot 150 mm x 80 mm x 12 mm with four M10 threaded inserts (but keep as through-holes 11 mm for this CAD prompt). One integral forward-facing yoke for the helm pedestal: 80 mm OD x 200 mm long stub at the beam center with a cross-hole 14 mm diameter. Bounding envelope 2050 mm x 200 mm x 120 mm. Material 6082-T6 marine alloy with E 70 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.33, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield 250 MPa, UTS 295 MPa, derated marine endurance limit 65 MPa at 10^7 cycles (ABYC H-8 marine aluminum fatigue class). Three load cases. Load Case 1 (cruise + chop): 1.5g vertical distributed on the beam = self weight + 140 kg (driver + helm + 90 kg battery) x 1.5 = 2.1 kN distributed; lateral 0.3g at all masses = 420 N lateral; both hull-mounting flanges fully fixed. Load Case 2 (asymmetric wave hit): one hull lifts clear, producing a pure bending moment at the captive hull pad of 2800 N-m (140 kg * 2g * 1.0 m offset from hull centerline), opposite flange free; evaluate as cantilever moment. Load Case 3 (crash stop from 15 knots): longitudinal 1.5 kN forward inertia on each battery foot (driver + battery + helm mass decelerating 10 m/s in 0.5 s = 2.0 m/s^2 x 140 kg = 280 N, amplified by 5x structural dynamic factor to 1.5 kN) applied rearward, both hull flanges fixed. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 cruise, peak von Mises must not exceed 125 MPa (yield/2.0 ABYC H-8 marine allowable); (R2) under LC2 asymmetric wave (highly dynamic), peak stress must not exceed 167 MPa (yield/1.5 transient); (R3) under LC3 crash stop, peak stress 167 MPa; (R4) fatigue: a 10^6 cycle wave spectrum with stress range 40% of LC1 peak, local stress range at any flange fillet or battery-foot transition must remain below 65 MPa; (R5) a linear buckling eigenvalue analysis under LC2 (cantilever compression on the top face) must yield a first-mode factor of at least 3.0; (R6) crossbeam mass must not exceed 18 kg to preserve boat displacement margin (limit derived from one-design hull displacement reserve); (R7) first two bending natural frequencies must exceed 15 Hz to avoid dominant wave-encounter frequencies 0.5-5 Hz and pump/motor excitation 8-12 Hz at cruise. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 geometry plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic 6082-T6 marine-grade aluminum crossbeam for a collegiate electric catamaran (energy class, one-design hulls, <=10 kWh energy budget, 2.0 m hull-to-hull beam). The crossbeam is a single machined-and-extruded billet, 2000 mm long with a 120 x 100 x 6 mm RHS, end flange pads bolting to hull deck inserts, two integral battery-tray feet on top, and a forward helm-pedestal yoke at the center. Apply ABYC H-8 marine-aluminum allowables (yield/2.0 continuous, yield/1.5 transient).

Envelope: ~2050 x 200 x 120 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 cruise + chop: 1.5 g vertical distributed (self-weight + 140 kg driver/helm/battery = 2.1 kN) plus 0.3 g lateral = 420 N, both hull flanges fixed.
- LC2 asymmetric wave hit: one hull lifts clear, producing a 2800 N-m cantilever moment at the captive flange (140 kg * 2 g * 1.0 m offset).
- LC3 crash stop from 15 knots: 1.5 kN longitudinal at each battery foot, both hull flanges fixed.
Also check a 10^6-cycle wave-spectrum fatigue against ABYC H-8 marine-aluminum endurance, first-mode buckling under LC2 cantilever compression, and first two bending modes above 15 Hz (wave-encounter 0.5-5 Hz, pump/motor 8-12 Hz).

prompt.title: Collegiate energy-class electric catamaran cockpit crossbeam benchmark
prompt.brief: Single-billet 6082-T6 crossbeam 2.0 m long with integral hull pads, battery feet, and helm yoke; ABYC H-8 marine SF.

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Slamming loads reduced to equivalent static load per LC2.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Battery crash-retention per ABYC E-11 EV supplement
